info.title: TON HTTP API C++
info.description:
This API enables HTTP access to TON blockchain - getting accounts and wallets information, looking up blocks and transactions, sending messages to the blockchain, calling get methods of smart contracts, and more.

In addition to REST API, all methods are available through a JSON-RPC endpoint  with `method` equal to method name and `params` passed as a dictionary.

The response contains a JSON object, which always has a boolean field `ok` and either `error` or `result`. If `ok` equals true, the request was successful and the result of the query can be found in the `result` field. In case of an unsuccessful request, `ok` equals false and the error is explained in the `error`.

API Key should be sent either as `api_key` query parameter or `X-API-Key` header

get.description: Returns the current state of any account on the TON blockchain. Includes the balance (in nanotons), smart contract code and data (if deployed), account status, and a reference to the last transaction. This is the primary endpoint for checking if an address exists and what's deployed there.

200.description: The complete current state of the requested account.

get.description: Returns detailed account information with parsed contract state. For wallet contracts, identifies the wallet version (v3, v4, v5) and extracts wallet-specific fields like `seqno` and public key. For other contracts, returns the raw state.

200.description: Returns detailed account information including parsed contract state and type.

get.description: Returns wallet-specific information for an address. If the address is a known wallet contract, returns the wallet type, current `seqno` (needed for sending transactions), and wallet_id. Always check `wallet: true` before using wallet-specific fields. Call this before sending any transaction to get the current `seqno`.

200.description: Returns wallet-specific details: type, `seqno`, subwallet ID, and balance.

get.description: Returns the TON balance of an account in nanotons. 1 TON = 1,000,000,000 nanotons. A lightweight endpoint that returns only the balance without contract code, data, or other account details. Returns "0" for addresses that have never received any funds.

200.description: Returns the account balance in nanotons.

get.description: Returns the lifecycle state of an account. Possible values: `uninitialized` (address has no deployed contract but can receive TON), `active` (contract is deployed and working), `frozen` (contract suspended due to zero balance, send TON to unfreeze). Refer to the [account lifecycle states documentation](/foundations/status) for details. Check this before interacting with a contract.

200.description: Returns the account lifecycle state: `uninit`, `active`, or `frozen`. Refer to the [account lifecycle states documentation](/foundations/status) for details.

get.description: Returns metadata for Jetton or NFT contracts. Automatically detects the contract type and returns appropriate fields. For Jetton masters: total supply, admin, metadata. For Jetton wallets: balance, owner. For NFT items: collection, owner, content. For NFT collections: item count, metadata.

200.description: Returns parsed token metadata for Jetton masters, Jetton wallets, NFT collections, and NFT items.

get.description: Returns a summary of transactions in a specific block. Each item contains the account address and transaction ID, but not full transaction details. Use `count` to limit results and `after_lt`/`after_hash` for pagination. Call getTransactions with each transaction ID to get full details.

200.description: Returns short transaction identifiers (account, lt, hash) for transactions in the specified block.

get.description: Returns full transaction objects for all transactions in a specific block. Each transaction includes complete data: inbound and outbound messages, fees, state changes, and BoC-encoded raw data. Use `count` to limit results and `after_lt`/`after_hash` for pagination when `incomplete` is true.

200.description: Returns full transaction objects for transactions in the specified block.

get.description: Returns transaction history for an account. Transactions are returned newest-first. Each transaction shows the incoming message that triggered it, all outgoing messages, and fees paid. For pagination: use the `lt` and `hash` from the oldest transaction as the starting point for the next request.

parameters.description: SHA-256 transaction hash to start pagination from

200.description: Returns a list of transactions for the specified account, ordered by logical time.

get.description: Returns transaction history for an account in a standardized format. Transactions are returned newest-first. Each transaction includes the triggering inbound message, all outbound messages, and fees paid. The response includes a `previous_transaction_id` cursor for paginating through older transactions.

parameters.description: SHA-256 transaction hash to start pagination from

200.description: Returns transactions in standardized format with a pagination cursor.

get.description: Finds a transaction by message parameters. Given a source address, destination address, and message creation time (created_lt), returns the transaction that processed this message. Useful for locating when a previously sent message was executed.

200.description: Returns the transaction matching the given source, destination, and created logical time.

get.description: Finds the transaction that received a specific message. Given message parameters, returns the transaction on the destination account that processed the incoming message. Use this to trace message delivery across accounts.

200.description: Returns the resulting transaction on the destination account for the given message.

get.description: Finds the transaction that sent a specific message. Given message parameters, returns the transaction on the source account that created this outgoing message. Useful for tracing where a message originated from.

200.description: Returns the originating transaction on the source account for the given message.

get.description: Returns the current state of the TON masterchain. The `last` field contains the latest block used for querying current state. The `seqno` in `last` is the current block height. Use this endpoint to obtain the latest block reference for other queries.

200.description: The current masterchain state, including the latest block identifier, its state root hash, and the genesis block reference. Use `result.last.seqno` as the current block height.

get.description: Returns validator signatures for a specific masterchain block. Each signature proves that a validator approved this block. Use this for building cryptographic proofs or verifying block authenticity in trustless applications.

200.description: Returns validator signatures for the specified masterchain block.

get.description: Returns a Merkle proof that links a shardchain block to a masterchain block. This proof cryptographically verifies that the shard block is part of the canonical chain. Used by light clients and cross-chain bridges to verify shard data without trusting the API.

parameters.name: from_seqno
parameters.description: Seqno of masterchain block starting from which proof is required. If not specified latest masterchain block is used

200.description: Returns a cryptographic proof chain linking a shard block to the masterchain.

get.description: Returns the latest block that has reached consensus and is guaranteed to be final. This block will never be reverted, making it safe for confirming transactions that require absolute finality.

200.description: The latest masterchain block that has achieved finality, guaranteed to never be reverted. Returns its sequence number and Unix timestamp.

get.description: Finds a block by position or time. Specify workchain and shard, then provide either: `seqno` (exact block number), lt (find block containing this logical time), or unixtime (find block closest to this timestamp). Returns the full block ID including hashes needed for verification.

200.description: Returns the full block identifier matching the given `seqno`, logical time, or unix timestamp.

get.description: Returns the active shardchain block identifiers at a given masterchain block height. Each shard processes a subset of accounts in parallel. The response shows how the basechain is currently partitioned and which block each shard is at.

200.description: Returns the list of active shard block identifiers at the specified masterchain block.

get.description: Returns block metadata without the full transaction list. Includes timestamps, validator info, and references to previous blocks. Intended for block explorers and other use cases that require block information without transactions.

200.description: Returns the block header with merge/split flags, validator info, and timing data.

get.description: Returns the current size of the outbound message queue for each shard. A growing queue indicates network congestion. If the queue is large, transactions may take longer to process. Monitor this to detect network issues.

200.description: Outgoing message queue sizes for all active shards. A growing queue indicates network congestion and may cause transaction delays.

post.description: Executes a read-only method on a smart contract. Get methods query contract state without sending a transaction. Common methods include `seqno` (wallet sequence number), `get_wallet_data` (wallet info), and `get_jetton_data` (token info). Method arguments are provided in the `stack` array.

200.description: Returns the TVM stack output and exit code from executing the specified get method.

post.description: Executes a read-only method on a smart contract using typed stack entries. Input and output stack entries use explicit types (`TvmStackEntryNumber`, `TvmStackEntryCell`, etc.) for structured input/output handling. Common methods: `seqno` (wallet sequence number), `get_wallet_data` (wallet info), `get_jetton_data` (token info).

200.description: Returns the get method result in standardized stack format.

post.description: Broadcasts a signed message to the TON network. The `boc` parameter must contain a complete, signed external message in base64 format. The API validates the message and forwards it to validators. Returns immediately after acceptance; use getTransactions to confirm the transaction was processed.

200.description: Returns a success confirmation after broadcasting the serialized message to the network.

post.description: Broadcasts a signed message to the TON network and returns the message hash. The `boc` parameter must contain a complete, signed external message in base64 format. The API validates the message and forwards it to validators. The returned hash can be used to track the message's processing status.

200.description: Returns the message hash after broadcasting the serialized message to the network.

post.description: Calculates the fees required to send a message. Provide the destination address and message body. For new contract deployments, also include init_code and init_data. Set ignore_chksig to true when estimating before signing. Returns a breakdown of storage, gas, and forwarding fees.

200.description: Returns the estimated fees (gas, storage, forwarding) for the specified message.

get.description: Validates an address and returns it in all standard formats. Use this to convert between address formats or to validate user input. Returns raw format (0:abc), base64 bounceable (EQ), base64 non-bounceable (UQ), and URL-safe variants.

200.description: Returns the address in all supported formats (raw, bounceable, non-bounceable) with base64 and URL-safe variants.

get.description: Validates a hash and returns it in all standard formats. Use this to convert between hex (64 chars) and base64 (44 chars) representations. Works with any 256-bit hash including transaction hashes, block hashes, and message hashes.

200.description: Returns the hash in all supported formats (hex, base64, URL-safe base64).

get.description: Converts a raw address to user-friendly base64 format. Raw addresses use the format `workchain:hex` (e.g., `0:abc...`). The packed format is shorter and includes a checksum for error detection.

200.description: Returns the address converted to user-friendly (base64) format.

get.description: Converts a user-friendly base64 address back to its raw components. Returns the workchain ID, account hex, and flags indicating if the address is bounceable or testnet-only.

200.description: Returns the unpacked address components: workchain, hex account ID, and format flags.

get.description: Returns a specific blockchain configuration parameter. TON stores all network settings on-chain as numbered parameters. Common ones: 0 (config contract), 1 (elector), 15 (election timing), 17 (stake limits), 20-21 (gas prices), 34 (current validators). Check TON documentation for the full list.

200.description: Returns the specified blockchain configuration parameter as a TVM cell.

get.description: Returns all blockchain configuration parameters at once. Includes gas prices, validator settings, workchain configs, and governance rules. Use the optional `seqno` to get historical configuration at a specific block height.

200.description: Returns all blockchain configuration parameters as a single TVM cell.

get.description: Returns smart contract library code by hash. Some contracts reference shared libraries instead of including all code directly. When a library reference appears in contract code, this endpoint fetches the actual library implementation.

200.description: Returns the code of the requested shared library cells.

post.description: All API methods are available through this single endpoint using JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol. Send the method name in the `method` field and parameters as a dictionary in `params`. Useful for calling multiple methods in sequence or when JSON-RPC is preferred over REST.

200.description: Returns the result of the specified JSON-RPC method call.

workchain.name: workchain
workchain.description: The workchain to query. Use `-1` for masterchain (validators, system contracts, config) or `0` for basechain (regular accounts and contracts). Most user transactions happen on workchain `0`.

shard.name: shard
shard.description: The shard identifier. Masterchain always uses `-9223372036854775808`. For basechain, shards split and merge dynamically. Use the `shards` endpoint to discover current shard configuration.

seqno.name: seqno
seqno.description: Masterchain block sequence number (block height). Used to query state at a specific point in time. If omitted, returns the current state.

seqnoOptional.name: seqno
seqnoOptional.description: Query state at a specific block height. If omitted, returns the current state. Use this to look up historical data at a specific point in time.

rootHashOptional.name: root_hash
rootHashOptional.description: The block's root hash for verification. Together with `file_hash`, this uniquely and cryptographically identifies a block. Only needed when proof of block identity is required.

fileHashOptional.name: file_hash
fileHashOptional.description: The block's file hash for verification. Together with `root_hash`, this provides cryptographic proof of block identity. Only needed for trustless verification.

afterLtOptional.name: after_lt
afterLtOptional.description: Pagination cursor. Pass the `lt` value from the last item in the previous response to get the next page. Transactions and messages are ordered by logical time.

afterAccountHashOptional.name: after_hash
afterAccountHashOptional.description: Secondary pagination cursor for block transactions. When multiple accounts have transactions at the same `lt`, use this to continue from a specific account.

countOptional.name: count
countOptional.description: Maximum number of items to return. The default is 40. Use smaller values for faster responses or larger values to reduce the number of API calls.

limitOptional.name: limit
limitOptional.description: Maximum number of transactions to return. The default is 10. For accounts with many transactions, use pagination (lt + hash) to fetch more.

ltOptional.name: lt
ltOptional.description: Starting point for transaction history. Pass the lt from `last_transaction_id` to start from the most recent, or from a specific transaction to continue pagination.

toLtOptional.name: to_lt
toLtOptional.description: Stop fetching when reaching this logical time. Use this to limit the time range of returned transactions. Set to 0 (default) to fetch all available history.

archivalOptional.name: archival
archivalOptional.description: Request data from archival nodes. Regular nodes keep at least the last 2 days of history. Set to true when querying old transactions or historical state. Archival requests may be slower.

createdLt.name: created_lt
createdLt.description: The logical time when the message was created. Available in the `created_lt` field of message objects.

APIKeyHeader.name: X-API-Key
APIKeyHeader.description: API key header of the form `X-API-Key: <token>`, where `<token>` is the API key. Requests without a key are limited to 1 RPS. Refer to the [authentication guide](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-authentication) for details.

APIKeyQuery.name: api_key
APIKeyQuery.description: API key query parameter of the form `?api_key=<token>`, where `<token>` is the API key. Equivalent to the header method. Refer to the [authentication guide](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-authentication) for details.

EmptyRequest.description: Empty request body for endpoints that require no parameters.

AddressRequest.description: Request containing a single address parameter.

seqno.description: Masterchain block sequence number. Query the account state as it was at this block height. If omitted, returns the current state.
Masterchain block sequence number (block height). Used to query blockchain config at a specific block. If omitted, the latest masterchain block is used.

hash.description: Hash value to detect encoding formats for.

workchain.description: Workchain ID: `-1` for masterchain, `0` for basechain. Most user transactions are on workchain `0`.

shard.description: Shard identifier. A signed 64-bit integer. Masterchain uses -9223372036854775808.

seqno.description: Shardchain block sequence number. Identifies a specific block within the given workchain and shard. Use together with `workchain` and `shard` to uniquely identify a block.

from_seqno.description: Starting masterchain block for proof generation.

workchain.description: Workchain ID: `-1` for masterchain, `0` for basechain. Most user transactions are on workchain `0`.

shard.description: Shard identifier. A signed 64-bit integer. Masterchain uses -9223372036854775808.

seqno.description: Block sequence number within the specified workchain and shard. Use together with `workchain` and `shard` to uniquely identify a block. If omitted, the latest block is used.

lt.description: Logical time of this event. A globally unique counter that orders all blockchain events. Higher values are more recent.

LookupBlockRequest.description: Request to find a block by workchain, shard, and either `seqno`, lt, or unixtime.

workchain.description: Workchain ID: `-1` for masterchain, `0` for basechain. Most user transactions are on workchain `0`.

shard.description: Shard identifier. A signed 64-bit integer. Masterchain uses -9223372036854775808.

seqno.description: Block sequence number within the specified workchain and shard. Use together with `workchain` and `shard` to uniquely identify a block. If omitted, the latest block is used.

root_hash.description: Merkle root hash of the block state tree. Used for cryptographic verification.

file_hash.description: Hash of the serialized block data. Together with `root_hash`, uniquely identifies a block.

workchain.description: Workchain ID: `-1` for masterchain, `0` for basechain. Most user transactions are on workchain `0`.

shard.description: Shard identifier. A signed 64-bit integer. Masterchain uses -9223372036854775808.

seqno.description: Block sequence number within the specified workchain and shard. Use together with `workchain` and `shard` to uniquely identify a block. If omitted, the latest block is used.

root_hash.description: Merkle root hash of the block state tree. Used for cryptographic verification.

file_hash.description: Hash of the serialized block data. Together with `root_hash`, uniquely identifies a block.

after_lt.description: Return items after this logical time (for pagination).

after_hash.description: Return items after this hash (for pagination within same `lt`).

count.description: Maximum number of items to return.

BlockTransactionsRequest.description: Request to fetch transactions from a specific block with optional pagination.

lt.description: Logical time of this event. A globally unique counter that orders all blockchain events. Higher values are more recent.

hash.description: Hash of the starting transaction for pagination. Use together with `lt` from a previous response's `transaction_id`.

to_lt.description: Stop returning items at this logical time.

archival.description: Whether to use archival nodes for old data.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return.

TransactionsRequest.description: Request to fetch transaction history for an account with optional pagination and filtering parameters.

source.description: Sender address. Empty string for external messages (sent from outside the blockchain).

created_lt.description: Logical time when this message was created. Use with source and destination to uniquely identify a message.

config_id.description: Configuration parameter number. Each number controls different blockchain settings.

seqno.description: Masterchain block sequence number (block height). Used to query blockchain config at a specific block. If omitted, the latest masterchain block is used.
Masterchain block sequence number (block height). Used to query blockchain config at a specific block. If omitted, the latest masterchain block is used.

libraries.description: Array of library cell hashes to retrieve. Each hash is a 256-bit value in hex (64 chars) or base64 (44 chars) format.

boc.description: Serialized external message in BoC format, base64 encoded. Contains the signed transaction to broadcast.

SendBocRequest.description: Request to broadcast a signed message. The boc field contains the base64-encoded external message.

body.description: Message body in BoC format, base64 encoded.

init_code.description: Contract code for deployment messages, BoC format, base64 encoded. Omit for regular transfers.

init_data.description: Initial contract storage for deployment, BoC format, base64 encoded. Omit for regular transfers.

ignore_chksig.description: Set to `true` to skip signature verification during fee estimation; otherwise `false`. Useful when a real signature is not yet available.

EstimateFeeRequest.description: Request to estimate transaction fees. Include the target address, message body, and optionally init code/data for contract deployment.

id.description: Request identifier. Echoed back in the response for matching async calls.

params.description: Method parameters as a JSON object with key-value pairs.

JsonRpcRequest.description: A JSON-RPC 2.0 request. Set `method` to the API method name (e.g., "getWalletInformation") and `params` to a dictionary of parameters.

TonAddr.description: Account address in [raw](/foundations/addresses/formats#raw-format) format (e.g., `0:ca6e321c...`) or [user-friendly](/foundations/addresses/formats#user-friendly-format) format (e.g., `EQDKbjIcfM...`). All formats are automatically detected.

TonAddrWithoutWorkchain.description: The account ID portion of an address without the workchain prefix. This is the 256-bit identifier in hex or base64 format.

TonHash.description: A 256-bit hash value. Accepts either hex format (64 characters) or base64 format (44 characters). Used for block hashes, transaction hashes, and cryptographic proofs.

TonHashHex.description: A 256-bit hash in hexadecimal format. Exactly 64 characters (0-9, a-f).

Int256.description: A large integer represented as a string to avoid precision loss. Used for balances and other values that may exceed JavaScript's safe integer limit.

Bytes.description: Binary data encoded as base64. Used for serialized cells (BoC format), message bodies, and smart contract code/data.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for account address objects. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

account_address.description: The account address in raw format (`workchain:hex`), e.g. `0:abc123...`. Use the [pack address](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2/utils/pack-address) endpoint to convert to user-friendly base64 format.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for full block identifiers. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

workchain.description: Workchain ID: `-1` for masterchain, `0` for basechain.

shard.description: Shard identifier as a signed 64-bit integer string. Masterchain uses `-9223372036854775808`.

seqno.description: Block sequence number within its workchain and shard. For the masterchain (workchain `-1`), this equals the global block height. For basechain shards (workchain `0`), this is the sequence number local to that specific shard, not a global height.

root_hash.description: Merkle root hash of the block state tree.

file_hash.description: Hash of the serialized block file. Together with `root_hash`, uniquely identifies a block.

TonBlockIdExt.description: A complete block identifier with cryptographic hashes. Contains `workchain`, `shard`, `seqno` (position) plus `root_hash` and `file_hash` (verification).

DetectAddressBase64Variant.description: User-friendly address in both standard base64 and URL-safe base64 encodings.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for base64 address encoding variants. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

b64.description: Address in standard base64 encoding (48 characters, uses `+` and `/`).

b64url.description: Address in URL-safe base64 encoding (48 characters, uses `-` and `_` instead of `+` and `/`).

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for extra currency balance entries. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

id.description: A 32-bit integer identifying the extra currency. Currency IDs are defined at the blockchain configuration level and stored in `ConfigParam 7` of the masterchain. Refer to the [extra currency minting documentation](https://docs.ton.org/v3/documentation/infra/minter-flow) for details.

amount.description: Currency balance amount as a decimal string.

InternalTransactionId.description: A reference to a specific transaction. The combination of `lt` (logical time) and `hash` uniquely identifies any transaction. Use these values for pagination and lookups.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for transaction ID references. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

lt.description: Logical time of this transaction. A globally unique counter that orders all events on the blockchain.

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of this transaction, encoded in base64.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for raw (unrecognized) contract states. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

code.description: Smart contract code in BoC format, base64 encoded. Empty if account is uninitialized.

data.description: Smart contract persistent storage in BoC format, base64 encoded.

frozen_hash.description: Hash of the frozen state. Only present for frozen accounts.

AccountStateRaw.title: Raw account state
AccountStateRaw.description: Unrecognized or non-wallet contract state. Identified by `@type: raw.accountState`. Returned when TonLib cannot parse the contract as a known type.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for Wallet v3 contracts. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

wallet_id.description: Subwallet ID for v3+ wallets. Allows creating multiple wallets from one key. The default value is `0x29a9a317` (698983191) on both mainnet and testnet, derived from the first 4 bytes of the TON mainnet initial state hash. Note: v5 wallets use a different scheme with network-specific values to prevent cross-network transaction replay. Refer to the [wallet interaction guide](/standard/wallets/interact) for full details.

seqno.description: Wallet contract sequence number. Used for replay protection: each outgoing transaction must include the current `seqno` and increments it by 1. Fetch the current value before sending a transaction.

AccountStateWalletV3.title: Wallet v3
AccountStateWalletV3.description: Standard wallet contract version 3. Identified by `@type: wallet.v3.accountState`. The most widely deployed wallet type prior to v4.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for Wallet v4 contracts. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

wallet_id.description: Subwallet ID for v3+ wallets. Allows creating multiple wallets from one key. The default value is `0x29a9a317` (698983191) on both mainnet and testnet, derived from the first 4 bytes of the TON mainnet initial state hash. Note: v5 wallets use a different scheme with network-specific values to prevent cross-network transaction replay. Refer to the [wallet interaction guide](/standard/wallets/interact) for full details.

seqno.description: Wallet contract sequence number. Used for replay protection: each outgoing transaction must include the current `seqno` and increments it by 1. Fetch the current value before sending a transaction.

AccountStateWalletV4.title: Wallet v4
AccountStateWalletV4.description: Standard wallet contract version 4. Identified by `@type: wallet.v4.accountState`. Adds plugin support over v3.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for Highload Wallet v1 contracts. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

wallet_id.description: Subwallet ID for v3+ wallets. Allows creating multiple wallets from one key. The default value is `0x29a9a317` (698983191) on both mainnet and testnet, derived from the first 4 bytes of the TON mainnet initial state hash. Note: v5 wallets use a different scheme with network-specific values to prevent cross-network transaction replay. Refer to the [wallet interaction guide](/standard/wallets/interact) for full details.

seqno.description: Wallet contract sequence number. Used for replay protection: each outgoing transaction must include the current `seqno` and increments it by 1. Fetch the current value before sending a transaction.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for Jetton master contract data. Refer to the [token type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types#token-types-ton-center-extensions) for a full list.

address.description: Contract address of this Jetton master.

contract_type.description: Type of token contract: jetton_master, jetton_wallet, nft_collection, or nft_item.

total_supply.description: Total tokens in circulation, in the smallest unit. Divide by 10^decimals for human-readable amount.

mintable.description: Returns `true` if new tokens can still be minted; `false` means the supply is permanently capped.

admin_address.description: Admin address that can mint tokens or update metadata. Empty if admin rights were revoked.

jetton_content.description: Token metadata: name, symbol, decimals, description, and image URL.

jetton_wallet_code.description: Code used to deploy individual user wallets for this token. Used to verify wallet authenticity.

JettonMasterData.description: Jetton (fungible token) master contract data. Contains total supply, whether minting is allowed, admin address, and token metadata (name, symbol, decimals). This is the central contract that tracks the token.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for Jetton wallet contract data. Refer to the [token type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types#token-types-ton-center-extensions) for a full list.

address.description: Contract address of this Jetton wallet.

contract_type.description: Type of token contract: jetton_master, jetton_wallet, nft_collection, or nft_item.

balance.description: The token balance in the Jetton's base units (smallest denomination). To get the human-readable amount, divide by 10^decimals, where `decimals` is a metadata field on the Jetton master contract (commonly 9, but varies per token). Refer to the [Jetton overview](/standard/tokens/jettons/overview) for how Jetton decimals are defined.

owner.description: Wallet owner's address (the user who holds these tokens).

jetton.description: Address of the Jetton master contract this wallet belongs to.

mintless_is_claimed.description: Returns `true` if the mintless jetton allocation has been claimed; otherwise `false`.

jetton_wallet_code.description: Wallet contract code. Should match the master contract's wallet code to verify authenticity.

JettonWalletData.description: Jetton wallet contract data. Each user has a separate wallet contract for each token they hold. Contains the token balance, owner address, and reference to the master contract.

ok.title: Ok
ok.description: Returns `true` if the request succeeded; otherwise `false`. See the `error` field for details.

result.description: The method return value. The structure depends on the method called; see individual method documentation for response schemas. Only present when `ok` is `true`.

@extra.title: Extra information
@extra.description: Optional request ID that can be passed in the request and received back in the response. Useful for matching async responses.

id.description: Echoed request identifier from the original JSON-RPC request.

TonlibErrorResponse.description: The `ok` field is always false, `code` contains the HTTP status, and `error` describes what went wrong.

ok.description: Always `false` for error responses.

code.description: HTTP status code. Common values: 404 (not found), 409 (conflict), 422 (validation error), 429 (rate limit), 500 (server error), 504 (timeout), 542 (liteserver error).

error.description: Error message explaining what went wrong.

@extra.description: Extra data passed through from the request.

id.description: Request ID. Pass any string and it will be echoed in the response.
Request ID. Pass any string and it will be echoed in the response.

DetectAddress.description: Detected address in all supported formats. Contains the raw on-chain form, bounceable and non-bounceable user-friendly representations (each in base64 and URL-safe base64), the format type of the original input, and the testnet-only flag from the user-friendly encoding.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for address detection results. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

raw_form.description: Raw address in `workchain_id:account_id` hex format (e.g., `0:abc...def`). This is the canonical on-chain representation without flags or checksum.

bounceable.description: Bounceable form of the address in standard base64 and URL-safe base64. Bounceable addresses (prefix `E...`) instruct wallet software to set the bounce flag on outgoing messages, so funds are returned if the destination contract cannot process them.

non_bounceable.description: Non-bounceable form of the address in standard base64 and URL-safe base64. Non-bounceable addresses (prefix `U...`) instruct wallet software to clear the bounce flag, ensuring funds are credited even if the recipient has no deployed contract.

given_type.description: The encoding format of the address as provided in the request (e.g., `raw_form`, `dns`, `friendly_bounceable`, `friendly_non_bounceable`).

test_only.description: Returns `true` if the user-friendly address has the testnet-only flag set (`0x80`); otherwise `false`. This is a flag in the address encoding that tells wallet software to reject this address on mainnet. The underlying account itself can exist on both networks.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for hash detection results. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

b64.title: base64 form
b64.description: Hash in standard base64 encoding (44 characters, uses `+` and `/`).

b64url.title: base64 url-safe form
b64url.description: Hash in URL-safe base64 encoding (44 characters, uses `-` and `_` instead of `+` and `/`).

hex.title: hex form
hex.description: Hash in lowercase hexadecimal encoding (64 characters).

DetectHash.description: Detected hash in all supported encoding formats: hex (64 characters), standard base64 (44 characters), and URL-safe base64.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for full account state responses. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

balance.description: The account balance in nanotons (the smallest unit of TON). 1 TON = 1,000,000,000 nanotons (10^9), so divide by 10^9 to get the human-readable TON amount. Refer to the [units and decimals guide](/ecosystem/wallet-apps/deep-links#read-more) for more context. If this field appears on a Jetton wallet, the balance is in the token's base units. Divide by 10^decimals, where `decimals` is defined by the Jetton master contract. Refer to the [Jetton overview](/standard/tokens/jettons/overview) for details on how Jetton decimals work.

extra_currencies.description: Array of non-TON currency balances held by this account. Each entry contains a currency ID (integer) and balance amount (decimal string). Empty array if the account holds no extra currencies.

last_transaction_id.description: Reference to the most recent transaction. Use as starting point for getTransactions.

block_id.description: Full block identifier where this event occurred.

code.description: Smart contract code in BoC format, base64 encoded.

data.description: Raw transaction or contract data in BoC (Bag of Cells) format, base64 encoded.

frozen_hash.description: Hash of the frozen contract state. Only present for frozen accounts.

sync_utime.description: Unix timestamp of the block from which this data was read.

state.description: Account state: uninit, active, or frozen.

suspended.description: Returns `true` if the account has been suspended by a governance decision; otherwise `false`.

AddressInformation.description: Raw account state including balance, code, data, and status. The code and data fields contain the smart contract in BoC format.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for wallet info responses. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

wallet.description: Returns `true` if this address is a recognized wallet contract type; otherwise `false`.

balance.description: Account balance in nanotons as a decimal string.

wallet_type.description: Wallet contract version (e.g., `wallet v4 r2`, `wallet v5 r1`). Empty if not a recognized wallet.

seqno.description: Wallet contract sequence number. Used for replay protection: each outgoing transaction must include the current `seqno` and increments it by 1. Fetch the current value before sending a transaction.

wallet_id.description: Subwallet ID for v3+ wallets. Allows creating multiple wallets from one key. The default value is `0x29a9a317` (698983191) on both mainnet and testnet, derived from the first 4 bytes of the TON mainnet initial state hash. Note: v5 wallets use a different scheme with network-specific values to prevent cross-network transaction replay. Refer to the [wallet interaction guide](/standard/wallets/interact) for full details.

is_signature_allowed.description: Returns `true` if external signatures are enabled (v5 wallets only); `false` means the wallet is controlled only by plugins.

WalletInformation.description: Information about a wallet account. The `wallet` field indicates if this is a known wallet type. If true, `wallet_type`, `seqno`, and `wallet_id` will be populated. Check `seqno` before sending transactions.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for masterchain state info. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

state_root_hash.description: SHA-256 Merkle root hash of the entire blockchain state at the latest block.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for block signature collections. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

id.description: Full block identifier for the signed block.

signatures.description: Array of validator signatures for this block. Each entry contains the validator's short node ID (base64 hash) and their Ed25519 signature (base64).

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for shard block proof chains. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

mc_id.description: Masterchain block used as trust anchor for the proof.

links.description: Array of cryptographic links forming a proof chain from the shard block to the masterchain. Each link contains a block identifier and a Merkle proof (base64).

mc_proof.description: Array of masterchain block link proofs. Each entry contains source and destination block identifiers with corresponding Merkle proofs (base64) and state proofs (base64).

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for consensus block info. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

consensus_block.description: Sequence number of the latest consensus block.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when the consensus block was created.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for shard listing responses. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

shards.description: Array of active shard block identifiers. Each entry contains `workchain` (integer), `shard` ID (string), `seqno` (integer), `root_hash` (base64/hex), and `file_hash` (base64/hex).

Shards.description: List of shardchain blocks at a specific masterchain height. Shows how the basechain is currently partitioned.

BlockHeader.description: Block metadata including creation time, validator info, and links to previous blocks. Does not include the transactions themselves.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for block header objects. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

id.description: Full block identifier including workchain, shard, `seqno`, and hashes.

global_id.description: Global network ID (`-239` for mainnet, `-3` for testnet).

after_merge.description: Returns `true` if this block was created immediately after a shard merge; otherwise `false`.

after_split.description: Returns `true` if this block was created immediately after a shard split; otherwise `false`.

before_split.description: Returns `true` if this shard will split after this block; otherwise `false`.

want_merge.description: Returns `true` if validators have signaled a preference to merge this shard; otherwise `false`.

want_split.description: Returns `true` if validators have signaled a preference to split this shard; otherwise `false`.

validator_list_hash_short.description: Short hash of the validator set active during this block.

catchain_seqno.description: Catchain sequence number used for validator consensus.

min_ref_mc_seqno.description: Minimum masterchain block `seqno` referenced by this block.

is_key_block.description: Returns `true` if this is a key block containing validator set changes or config updates; otherwise `false`.

prev_key_block_seqno.description: Sequence number of the previous key block.

start_lt.description: Logical time at the start of this block.

end_lt.description: Logical time at the end of this block. All transactions in this block have `lt` between `start_lt` and `end_lt`.

gen_utime.description: Unix timestamp when this block was generated.

prev_blocks.description: Array of previous block identifiers (`workchain`, `shard`, `seqno`, `root_hash`, `file_hash`). Usually contains one entry, but two after a shard merge.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for outgoing message queue size responses. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

shards.description: Array of per-shard queue sizes. Each entry contains the shard's block identifier and the number of outgoing messages waiting in its queue (integer).

ext_msg_queue_size_limit.description: Maximum allowed external message queue size before new messages are rejected.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for blockchain configuration responses. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

config.description: The requested configuration parameter value as a TVM cell.

ConfigInfo.description: Blockchain configuration data. Contains one or more config parameters as requested. Each parameter controls different aspects of network operation.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for library lookup results. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

result.description: Array of library entries. Each entry contains a hash (base64 or hex) identifying the library and a data field with the library code in BoC format (base64).

LibraryResult.description: Library code fetched by hash. Contains the actual code cells for shared libraries referenced by smart contracts.

BlockTransactions.description: List of short transaction identifiers found in a specific block, with a flag indicating whether more transactions are available for pagination.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for block transaction listings. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

id.description: Full block identifier for the queried block.

req_count.description: The maximum number of transactions requested, as specified by the `count` request parameter. If `incomplete` is `true`, more transactions exist beyond this limit.

incomplete.description: Returns `true` if there are more transactions in this block; otherwise `false`. Use the last transaction as cursor for pagination.

transactions.description: Array of compact transaction references. Each entry contains the account address (string), logical time (string), and transaction hash (base64 or hex).

BlockTransactionsExt.description: List of full transaction objects found in a specific block, with a flag indicating whether more transactions are available for pagination.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for extended block transaction listings. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

id.description: Full block identifier for the queried block.

req_count.description: The maximum number of transactions requested, as specified by the `count` request parameter. If `incomplete` is `true`, more transactions exist beyond this limit.

incomplete.description: Returns `true` if there are more transactions in this block; otherwise `false`. Use the last transaction as cursor for pagination.

transactions.description: Array of full transaction objects. Each entry contains the account address, timestamps, inbound/outbound messages, fees (in nanotons), and the raw transaction BoC (base64).

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for paginated raw transaction lists. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

transactions.description: Array of transaction objects in standardized format. Each transaction contains address, timestamps, inbound/outbound messages, fees in nanotons, and raw BoC (base64).

previous_transaction_id.description: Use this as cursor to fetch the next page of older transactions.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for external message info after broadcast. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the external message as accepted by the network.

hash_norm.description: SHA-256 normalized message hash, stable across re-serializations. Defined in [TEP-467: normalized message hash](https://github.com/ton-blockchain/TEPs/blob/master/text/0467-normalized-message-hash.md).

ExtMessageInfo.description: Information about a broadcast external message. Contains the message hash which can be used to track processing.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for successful operations with no return data. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

ResultOk.description: Simple success response with no additional data.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for fee breakdown objects. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

in_fwd_fee.description: Fee for importing this inbound message, in nanotons.

storage_fee.description: Storage fee charged for keeping the contract state on-chain, in nanotons.

gas_fee.description: Computation gas fee for executing contract code, in nanotons.

fwd_fee.description: Fee for forwarding outbound messages created by this transaction, in nanotons.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for fee estimation results. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

source_fees.description: Fees charged on the sending account.

destination_fees.description: Array of fee breakdowns charged on the receiving account(s). One entry per destination in multi-hop transactions.

QueryFees.description: Estimated fees for a transaction. Shows the breakdown between storage fees (for contract state), gas fees (for computation), and forward fees (for message delivery).

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for slice-typed stack entries. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.
TonLib type identifier for cell-typed stack entries. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.
TonLib type identifier for number-typed stack entries. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.
TonLib type identifier for tuple-typed stack entries. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.
TonLib type identifier for list-typed stack entries. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.
TonLib type identifier for stack entries with unsupported types. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.
TonLib type identifier for TVM slice values. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

bytes.description: TVM slice data serialized as base64.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for TVM cell values. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

bytes.description: TVM cell data serialized as base64.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for TVM decimal number values. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

number.description: Integer value as a decimal string (supports arbitrarily large numbers).

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for TVM tuple structures. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

elements.description: Ordered tuple of TVM stack entries. Each element is a typed object with `@type` discriminator (`tvm.stackEntryNumber`, `tvm.stackEntryCell`, `tvm.stackEntrySlice`, `tvm.stackEntryTuple`, or `tvm.stackEntryList`).

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for TVM list structures. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

elements.description: Ordered list of TVM stack entries. Each element is a typed object with `@type` discriminator (`tvm.stackEntryNumber`, `tvm.stackEntryCell`, `tvm.stackEntrySlice`, `tvm.stackEntryTuple`, or `tvm.stackEntryList`).

method.description: The get method name (e.g., "`seqno`", "get_wallet_data") or its numeric ID.

stack.description: Input arguments as a TVM stack. Each entry is a typed object with `@type` discriminator: `tvm.stackEntryNumber` (decimal string), `tvm.stackEntryCell` (base64 BoC), `tvm.stackEntrySlice` (base64 BoC), `tvm.stackEntryTuple`, or `tvm.stackEntryList`.

seqno.description: Masterchain block sequence number. Run the get method against the contract state at this specific block height. If omitted, uses the current state.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for standardized get method execution results. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

gas_used.description: Gas consumed during execution. Useful for cost estimation.

stack.description: Output values as a TVM stack. Each entry is a typed object with `@type` discriminator: `tvm.stackEntryNumber` (decimal string), `tvm.stackEntryCell` (base64 BoC), `tvm.stackEntrySlice` (base64 BoC), `tvm.stackEntryTuple`, or `tvm.stackEntryList`.

exit_code.description: TVM exit code. `0` or `1` means success; other values indicate errors. Refer to the [TVM exit code reference](/tvm/exit-codes#exit-codes) for all values.

RunGetMethodStdResult.description: Result of executing a smart contract get method. Contains the TVM exit code (0 or 1 means success), gas consumed, and the output stack with typed return values.

refs.description: Array of child cell references, forming a directed acyclic graph (DAG). Each child is a TVM cell object with its own data (base64), refs, and special flag.

special.description: Returns `true` if this is a special (exotic) cell type such as a Merkle proof or library reference; otherwise `false`.

object.description: Parsed cell object with data, refs, and type info.

method.description: The get method name (e.g., "`seqno`", "get_wallet_data") or its numeric ID.

stack.description: Input arguments as a TVM stack. Each entry is a two-element array: `[type, value]` where type is `"num"` (decimal string), `"cell"` (base64 BoC), or `"slice"` (base64 BoC).

seqno.description: Masterchain block sequence number. Run the get method against the contract state at this specific block height. If omitted, uses the current state.

RunGetMethodRequest.description: Request to execute a smart contract get method. Specify address, method name/ID, and input stack.

@type.description: TonLib type identifier for get method execution results. Refer to the [TonLib type reference](/ecosystem/api/toncenter/v2-tonlib-types) for a full list.

gas_used.description: Gas consumed during execution. Useful for cost estimation.

stack.description: Output values as a TVM stack. Each entry is a two-element array: `[type, value]` where type is `"num"` (decimal string), `"cell"` (base64 BoC), or `"slice"` (base64 BoC).

exit_code.description: TVM exit code. `0` or `1` means success; other values indicate errors. Refer to the [TVM exit code reference](/tvm/exit-codes#exit-codes) for all values.

block_id.description: Block at which the get method was executed.

last_transaction_id.description: Most recent transaction at the time of execution.

RunGetMethodResult.description: Result of executing a smart contract get method. Contains the TVM exit code (0 or 1 means success), gas used, and the output stack with return values.

UnpackedAddress.description: Decoded address components extracted from a packed base64 address. Contains the workchain, raw hex account ID, and flags for bounceable and testnet formats.

workchain.description: Workchain ID extracted from the address: `0` for basechain, `-1` for masterchain.

bounceable.description: Returns `true` if the user-friendly address was encoded in bounceable format (flag byte `0x11`); otherwise `false` (non-bounceable, flag byte `0x51`).

testnet.description: Returns `true` if the user-friendly address has the testnet-only flag set (flag byte includes `0x80`); otherwise `false`. This is a metadata flag in the address encoding. The underlying account can exist on both testnet and mainnet.

addr_hex.description: The 256-bit `account_id` in hexadecimal (64 characters). Combined with `workchain`, this forms the raw address.
